title: Flow Cytometry-Based Gamma-H2AX Assay for Radiation Biodosimetry
steps: Collect blood samples in heparinized Vacutainers (BD, USA).
Irradiate 2 ml aliquot of whole blood with the required radiation dose.
Incubate for 30 minutes (or other time) at 37ºC.
Take the 2 ml whole blood and dilute (1:1) with 2ml PBS in a 15 ml tube.
Carefully layer the mix on 2 ml of Histopaque (Ficoll).
Centrifuge at 1,200 g (Eppendorf 5810 R) for 15 min at RT.
Isolate middle cloudy lymphocytes layer and wash 2-3 times with cold PBS*.

Incubate lymphocytesfor 1 hour at 37ºC with primary antibodies (anti-phospho-histone H2A.X, Ser139, clone JBW301; Millipore, CA, USA), in a 1:200 dilution in a buffercontaining 1% bovine serum albumin (BSA) and 0.12 % TX-100.

Incubate withsecondary antibodies (FITC-goat antimouse Ig BD Pharmingen, San Jose, CA, USA),in a 1:200 dilution in a buffer containing 1% bovine serum albumin (BSA) and 12 % TX-100, for 1 hour at 37◦C.
Suspend lymphocytes in fresh 400-500 μl of PBS according to cells concentration.
Measure gamma-H2AXfluorescence in lymphocytes using flow cytometer (LSR II, Becton Dickinson, USA) and analyze the data using PC-basedBD FACSDiva software. The following settings are used:
Fix lymphocytes(1-5x10 6 ) with 2% paraformaldehyde (PFA) for 10-20 minutes on ice. Atthis stage, fixed lymphocytes can either be processed further forimmunostaining or stored overnight at +4 ºC .
FITC laser excitationof 488 nm and emission of 533 nm.
A single populationof lymphocytes is gated and used for mean fluorescence intensity (MFI) calculations.
Yields of γH2AX arecalculated as the ratio of mean fluorescence intensity of irradiated versuscontrol samples.
creator: Ghazi Alsbeih
